The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Juncus divaricatus include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Juncus divaricatus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems and skin conditions.
